source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - VK 900 A Platform Fire

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Viosca Knoll Block 900

actions_taken: Personnel attempted to extinguish fire using handheld and wheel unit fire extinguishers
Platform evacuation ordered by Person in Charge
All 6 personnel safely evacuated to muster station
Motor Vessel Landon James transported personnel to nearby facility
Personnel transported via helicopter to shore and onshore medical facility
M/V Ms. Emily and M/V Mr. Cade assisted with fire suppression using water cannons
BSEE preservation order issued and investigation conducted
event.summary: On 22 July 2023 at approximately 1240 hours, a fire occurred on VK 900 A Platform when diesel from a tote tank spilled onto the intake housing of the #1 turbine compressor. The fire was initiated by diesel contacting the hot turbine exhaust and/or an exposed extension cord. Personnel attempted firefighting but the fire spread to the 8-man sleeper and became uncontrollable. All 6 personnel were safely evacuated. The fire was extinguished by responding vessels using water cannons. Property damage was estimated at $1,900,000.
recommendations: Implement comprehensive hazard analysis and written job procedures for diesel transfer operations
Establish protocols for securing hoses and extension cords during work breaks
Improve inspection and maintenance of electrical equipment to prevent exposure and degradation
Ensure Job Safety Analysis (JSA) documents are retained and accessible for all maintenance and transfer operations

threats.name: Diesel spillage from tote tank during transfer operation
Siphoning effect causing uncontrolled diesel overflow
Diesel contact with hot turbine exhaust surface
Diesel contact with exposed electrical wiring

consequences.name: Fire initiation and rapid fire growth
Fire spread to adjacent structures (8-man sleeper)
Platform evacuation and personnel exposure to hazards
Property damage to equipment and structures

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for diesel transfer operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and establish safe work procedures
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent hazardous conditions

supporting_text: BSEE requested Cox provide the Job Safety Analysis's (JSA) used for the maintenance work performed on the Firewater/Jockey Pump, the chemical transfer process, and any other JSA performed on 22 July 2023. Cox was unable to provide the requested JSA's, stating that they were 'lost in the fire'.
Management Systems – No or inadequate hazard analysis/written job procedure: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for the transfer of diesel was not found.

controls.name: Secure hose and equipment placement during work breaks
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent unattended equipment hazards
controls.lod_basis: Work procedure to maintain safe conditions during operational pauses

supporting_text: Personnel left the hose used for the diesel transfer and extension cord out while they broke for lunch.
the discharge hose was removed from the tote tank and placed within the containment skid, and the suction hose was left in the day tank while operators broke for lunch.

controls.name: Inspection and maintenance of electrical extension cords
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent electrical ignition sources
controls.lod_basis: Equipment maintenance to ensure electrical safety

supporting_text: The extension cord going to the pump from the 8-man sleeper was laid on top of the grating. Signs of oxidation and decay were observed on the extension cord, suggesting that the cord had been stepped on while in use.
the exposed extension cord, which may have caused ignition.

controls.name: Containment skid design and overflow prevention
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent liquid spillage

supporting_text: It is suspected that the vacuum created by placing the discharge hose on the grating caused a siphoning effect from the day tank, resulting in diesel spilling out and overflowing the containment skid.

controls.name: Firewater/Jockey Pump operational status
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control for fire suppression capability
controls.lod_basis: Fire suppression system to control fire spread

supporting_text: The sludge in the day tank was causing the fuel filters on the generator powering the Firewater/Jockey Pump to clog. As a result, the Firewater/Jockey Pump was shut down at 1600 on 21 July 2023.

controls.name: Handheld and wheel unit fire extinguishers
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control for initial fire suppression
controls.lod_basis: Portable fire suppression equipment for personnel response

supporting_text: Personnel on Board (POB) attempted to extinguish the fire using handheld and wheel unit fire extinguishers.

POB continued using handheld and wheel unit fire extinguishers to fight the fire but were unsuccessful. After exhausting most of the fire extinguishers onboard, the PIC ordered all 6 POB to evacuate the platform.

controls.name: SCADA alarm system and fire detection
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control for fire detection and alert
controls.lod_basis: Automated detection system to alert personnel to fire

supporting_text: witnesses stated hearing 'Fire' over the radio followed by the fire alarm sounding. The PIC checked the Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) computer and saw that the alarm was from the #1 turbine compressor.

controls.name: Emergency shutdown (ESD) system
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to isolate hazardous operations
controls.lod_basis: Automated system to shut down operations during emergency

supporting_text: In response, the platform's emergency shut down (ESD) system was manually initiated

controls.name: Platform evacuation procedures and muster station
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control for personnel safety during emergency
controls.lod_basis: Evacuation protocol to remove personnel from hazard area

supporting_text: the PIC ordered all 6 POB to evacuate the platform. POB made their way to the muster station and donned their personal floatation devices.
All POB safely boarded the responding Motor Vessel (M/V) Landon James and were transported to a nearby production facility

controls.name: Personal floatation devices (PFD)
controls.barrier_role: PPE for personnel safety during evacuation
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment for water evacuation

supporting_text: POB made their way to the muster station and donned their personal floatation devices.

controls.name: Vessel response and external fire suppression
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control for large-scale fire suppression
controls.lod_basis: External resources for fire control when onboard systems fail

supporting_text: At approximately 1330 hours, M/V Ms. Emily and M/V Mr. Cade arrived on scene and assisted extinguishing the fire using their vessel's water cannons.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
